title: Travel & Tourism
subtitle: Essential Vocabulary and Grammar for Aptis Speaking & Writing

sections.title: Travel & Tourism - Level B2
sections.quote: Essential Vocabulary for Aptis Writing & Speaking about Travel

features.title: Travel Vocabulary
features.description: Essential terms for discussing destinations, accommodations, and experiences.

features.title: Cultural Exchange
features.description: Describing cultural experiences and intercultural communication.

features.title: Travel Industry
features.description: Professional language for tourism careers and travel planning.

badge.title: APTIS ALIGNED
badge.text: This module specifically addresses the B2 performance descriptors for lexical range and accuracy in travel contexts.

tip.title: Academic Insight
tip.text: Focus on descriptive adjectives and sensory language to make your travel descriptions more vivid and engaging.

sections.title: 1. Travel Vocabulary
sections.description: Click cards to flip and listen.

cards.definition: Views or landscapes that are extremely beautiful and impressive.
The experience of deeply engaging with and understanding another culture.
Referring to places that are not commonly visited by tourists.
cards.translation: Nơi hẻo lánh, ít người biết đến

cards.definition: Travel that respects local environments and communities while minimizing negative impact.
Traditional food and drink specialties of a particular region or country.
A planned schedule of activities and destinations for a trip.

tip.title: Pro-Tip
tip.text: Use "breathtaking" instead of "very beautiful" to create a stronger impression in your travel descriptions.

sections.title: 2. Travel Synonyms (B2 Level)
sections.description: Elevate your vocabulary by replacing common A1/A2 words with professional B2-level alternatives.

practiceTasks.label: Practice Task
practiceTasks.text: Rewrite your last travel description using at least three B2 equivalents from the table above.

practiceTasks.label: Speaking Drill
practiceTasks.text: Describe your dream vacation using at least five advanced vocabulary words from this section.

tip.title: Pro-Tip: Precision Matters
tip.text: In travel writing, using "picturesque" instead of "beautiful" specifically conveys that something is like a picture - visually appealing and charming.

sections.title: 3. Travel Grammar: Past Experiences
sections.description: Using past tenses effectively to describe travel experiences and memories.

points.title: Past Simple for Completed Actions
points.function: To describe completed travel experiences in chronological order
examples: I visited the Eiffel Tower last summer.
We stayed in a boutique hotel in Paris.

points.title: Past Continuous for Background Actions
points.function: To describe ongoing actions during which something else happened
examples: I was taking photos when the sun set behind the mountains.
They were exploring the market when they found the antique shop.

points.title: Present Perfect for Recent Experiences
points.function: To describe travel experiences that have relevance to the present
examples: I have traveled to over 15 countries.
She has never experienced such breathtaking scenery before.

rows: I visited three museums during my trip.
We were hiking when it started to rain.
I have explored many European cities.

tip.title: Pro-Tip: Tense Clarity
tip.text: Use past simple for specific completed trips, and present perfect for general travel experiences that may continue.

sections.title: Travel Vocabulary Practice
sections.description: Choose the best word or phrase to complete each travel sentence.

questions.prompt: "The ________ views from the mountain top were absolutely stunning."
"To truly understand a culture, you need to experience ________ with the local community."
"We decided to explore ________ areas instead of popular tourist destinations."
"________ focuses on minimizing environmental impact and supporting local communities."
"One of the highlights of my trip was trying the local ________ at traditional restaurants."
"Before our vacation, we spent weeks planning our travel ________."
"The hotel was a charming boutique ________ with excellent service."
"During our ________, we discovered a hidden waterfall in the jungle."
"The village was so ________ that it looked like it was from another century."
"This was a truly ________ experience that I will never forget."

sections.title: Travel Experience Matching
sections.description: Drag each travel experience to the correct category.

sections.title: Reading Part 2: Travel Planning
sections.description: Complete the passage using the most appropriate travel vocabulary.

passage.title: Planning the Perfect Adventure
parts: immersion with local communities. Don't forget to pack your camera to capture

questions.prompt: What is the main purpose of the passage?
options: To provide advice on travel planning

questions.prompt: According to the passage, what should travelers do to create lasting memories?

tip.title: Reading Tip
tip.text: Look for collocations such as "breathtaking views" and "memorable memories" to help you choose the correct words.

sections.title: Writing Part 4: Travel Experience
sections.description: Write a short formal response using the vocabulary from this lesson.

prompt.situation: You have been asked to write a short article for your school magazine about an unforgettable travel experience. Describe where you went, what you did, and why it was memorable. Use at least 5 vocabulary words from this lesson.
prompt.referenceAnswer: Last summer, I had the opportunity to experience cultural immersion in a picturesque village in Vietnam. The breathtaking scenery and warm hospitality of the locals made this an unforgettable adventure. I explored off the beaten path locations and tried the delicious local cuisine. This sustainable tourism experience taught me the importance of respecting local communities while traveling. It was truly a memorable journey that broadened my perspective on different cultures.

tip.title: B2 Writing Tip
tip.text: Use a variety of past tenses to make your travel narrative more engaging. Combine past simple for completed actions with past continuous for ongoing experiences.
